name: Thermographic Dungeon Scan
rationale: FLIR-inspired false-color infrared rendering turns every cave encounter into a classified threat-assessment feed — monsters pulse in heat-bloom orange and magenta against cold-blue stone, making the security red-team framing literal and instantly arresting on a scroll.
preamble: Digital art in a FLIR thermal-imaging aesthetic: false-color infrared palette of deep ultraviolet-black backgrounds, cold cobalt-blue stone and shadows, warm amber and electric-orange for bodies and torchlight, hot magenta-white cores at points of danger or magical energy. Hard scanline texture overlay suggesting a military sensor feed. Crisp vector-clean silhouettes with no traditional linework — forms defined entirely by temperature gradient and thermal bloom. Sparse white HUD annotations (distance markers, threat tags, heat-signature halos) in a monospace font. Mood: clinical, ominous, covert-ops. References: FLIR Systems military footage, Tom Clancy Ghost Recon thermal-vision cutscenes, Predator 1987 heat-vision sequences, 2026 dark-tech Instagram reels blending fantasy and surveillance aesthetics.
